questions.prompt: How do you say "I want to go to Beirut"?
questions.explanation: بدي (baddi) means 'I want' — so بدي روح = I want to go. رح روح = I will go (future). عم بروح = I am going (present continuous).
عم (ʕam) + بـ (b-) prefix marks the present continuous in Lebanese Arabic — something happening right now. رح بشوف = I will watch. كنت بشوف = I used to watch.
رح (raḥ) before the verb marks future tense in Lebanese Arabic. سـ (sa-) is the MSA future marker, not used in spoken Lebanese. بدي expresses wanting, not simple future.

questions.prompt: How do you say "I didn't understand"?
questions.explanation: ما فهمت (ma fhimt) uses the Lebanese negation ما + past tense verb فهمت (I understood). ما بعرف = I don't know. مش طبيعي = not normal.

options: I used to live in Lebanon
questions.explanation: كنت (kint) + بـ-prefix verb forms the past habitual — 'I used to...' So كنت بعيش = I used to live. رح عيش = I will live. عم بعيش = I am living.

questions.prompt: What does the word كبير mean?
questions.explanation: كبير (kbīr) means big or old (for age). زغير (zghīr) = small/young, طويل (ṭawīl) = tall, سريع (sarīʕ) = fast. In Lebanese Arabic, كبير بالعمر means 'old in age'.

questions.prompt: How do you say "quickly" in Lebanese Arabic?
questions.explanation: بسرعة (b-surʕa) means 'quickly'. بشوي (b-shwayy) = slowly/a little, ببطء (b-buṭ') is the MSA form of slowly. عالآخر = to the max. Lebanese commonly uses بسرعة in daily speech.
بياكل (byēkol) uses the بـ prefix for present/habitual tense — 'he eats' or 'he is eating'. أكل (akal) = he ate, رح ياكل = he will eat, بدو ياكل = he wants to eat.

questions.prompt: The preposition عـ in Lebanese Arabic means:
questions.explanation: عـ (ʕa-) is a shortened form of على meaning 'to' or 'at'. من (min) = from, مع (maʕ) = with, بـ (b-) = in. Example: رح روح عالبيت = I will go to the house.

questions.prompt: How do you say "before" in Lebanese Arabic?
questions.explanation: قبل (ʔabil) means 'before'. بعدين (baʕdēn) = later/afterwards, هلق (halla') = now, بكرا (bukra) = tomorrow. These time words are essential for forming sequences in conversation.

questions.prompt: Which sentence correctly says 'She is sleeping'?
questions.explanation: عم بتنام (ʕam bitnēm) uses عم + بـ prefix for present continuous — 'she is sleeping right now'. نامت = she slept, رح تنام = she will sleep, بدها تنام = she wants to sleep.
حلو (ḥilu) means sweet or beautiful/nice in Lebanese Arabic. It's used for both food (sweet taste) and people/things (pretty, nice). بشع = ugly, مر = bitter, مالح = salty.

questions.prompt: How do you say "with me" in Lebanese Arabic?
questions.explanation: معي (maʕi) means 'with me'. عندي (ʕindi) = I have / at my place, إلي (ili) = for me / I have, منّي (minni) = from me. These preposition + pronoun combos are essential in Lebanese.

title: Phase 2 Fill in the Blank — Verbs & Phrases

fillBlanks.sentence: To say "I am eating" in Lebanese, you say عم ___.
fillBlanks.hint: The b-prefix form of the verb أكل (to eat) for 'I'.

fillBlanks.sentence: The word ___ means "slowly" or "a little" in Lebanese.
fillBlanks.hint: Often used to tell someone to slow down or take it easy.

fillBlanks.sentence: رح ___ عالبيت means 'I will go home'.
fillBlanks.hint: The bare verb for 'go' after the future marker رح.

fillBlanks.sentence: The adjective ___ means "tired" in Lebanese Arabic.
fillBlanks.hint: How you feel after a long day — related to the root ت-ع-ب.

fillBlanks.sentence: بدي ___ مي means 'I want to drink water'.
fillBlanks.hint: The verb for 'drink' in its base form.
A time word for the present moment — uniquely Lebanese.

fillBlanks.sentence: The negation pattern in Lebanese is ___ + verb.
fillBlanks.hint: A short word placed before the verb to negate it.
Used to intensify adjectives — like كتير منيح (very good).
